title: project.create
description: Create a new project from confirmed Inventory items. The use case validates identity, resolves initial sources, builds a FilesystemPlan for folder + marker + tool scaffold, persists the project in 'setup_incomplete', and emits an audit event. The first lifecycle transition (setup_incomplete → ready) is owned by spec 009 and is NOT performed here.

ErrorCode.description: Project-create-scoped error codes layered on the spec 002 ErrorEnvelope.

message.description: Human-readable explanation. MUST NOT include secrets or absolute filesystem paths.

details.description: Structured context. name.duplicate MUST include conflictingProjectId. source.not_found MUST include the missing inventory ids. path.collision MUST include the colliding project id.

Request.description: Project creation request. initialSources is optional; an empty array creates the project skeleton without sources, which is valid (the project lands in setup_incomplete and the spec 009 ready transition is gated on a non-empty source list).

name.description: User-facing project label. Validated for non-emptiness, length, and uniqueness within the library.

path.description: Library-root-relative folder path for the new project. The use case verifies it is unused before planning the folder write.

initialSources.description: Inventory session ids to link at create time. Sources are picked from Inventory only (research R2); arbitrary disk paths are refused upstream by the UI picker.

canonicalTargetId.description: Optional canonical_target id to link at creation (spec 035).

lifecycle.description: New projects deterministically start in setup_incomplete. The first transition to 'ready' is owned by spec 009 and runs after the FilesystemPlan is applied.

planId.description: FilesystemPlan id for the folder + marker + tool scaffold. Present when the project required a filesystem write; absent for metadata-only creates (e.g. onboarding a folder whose marker already exists).

channels.description: Channels inferred from initialSources at create time. Empty when no sources were linked.
